title: Frequency and lookup patterns

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Frequency and lookup patterns; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Hash-based patterns trade memory for fast expected lookup in frequency counting, membership, indexing, grouping, and prefix-state matching.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A frequency map finds the first repeated value while preserving a separate traversal for order. Your goal is to recognize and solve lookup and frequency problems with justified expected complexity. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Array representation and operations; Linked lists; Resizing and expected complexity. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Hash-based patterns trade memory for fast expected lookup in frequency counting, membership, indexing, grouping, and prefix-state matching. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Ordering requirements, worst-case guarantees, key mutation, and bounded alphabets can favor other structures. The draft therefore rejects: A hash map is always better than an array for counting values.

includes: Use sets and maps for membership, counting, indexing and complements without unnecessary nested scans.
Recognize and solve lookup and frequency problems with justified expected complexity.

In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
excludes: Ordering requirements, worst-case guarantees, key mutation, and bounded alphabets can favor other structures.

outcomes: Recognize and solve lookup and frequency problems with justified expected complexity.

examples.title: Frequency and lookup patterns: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: A frequency map finds the first repeated value while preserving a separate traversal for order.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: A frequency map finds the first repeated value while preserving a separate traversal for order.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Choose hashing when key range is broad and expected lookup fits; bounded dense keys may favor an array.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Choose hashing when key range is broad and expected lookup fits; bounded dense keys may favor an array.

examples.title: Frequency and lookup patterns: implementation transfer
examples.situation: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Ordering requirements, worst-case guarantees, key mutation, and bounded alphabets can favor other structures.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.
